prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: automatic_top_up, balance_not_updated_after_bank_transfer, beneficiary_not_allowed, cancel_transfer, card_payment_fee_charged, card_payment_not_recognised, declined_cash_withdrawal, exchange_rate, exchange_via_app, failed_transfer, get_disposable_virtual_card, get_physical_card, passcode_forgotten, pending_top_up, pending_transfer, receiving_money, reverted_card_payment?, top_up_by_cash_or_cheque, top_up_reverted, topping_up_by_card, transfer_fee_charged, transfer_into_account, transfer_timing, verify_my_identity, verify_source_of_funds.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into exactly one of these categories: automatic_top_up, balance_not_updated_after_bank_transfer, beneficiary_not_allowed, cancel_transfer, card_payment_fee_charged, card_payment_not_recognised, declined_cash_withdrawal, exchange_rate, exchange_via_app, failed_transfer, get_disposable_virtual_card, get_physical_card, passcode_forgotten, pending_top_up, pending_transfer, receiving_money, reverted_card_payment?, top_up_by_cash_or_cheque, top_up_reverted, topping_up_by_card, transfer_fee_charged, transfer_into_account, transfer_timing, verify_my_identity, verify_source_of_funds.

Definitions and rules:
- automatic_top_up: About enabling, disabling, or issues with automatic balance top-ups.
- balance_not_updated_after_bank_transfer: Balance not reflecting after a (bank) transfer that has already been sent.
- beneficiary_not_allowed: About rejection or inability to add or send to a recipient/beneficiary, or beneficiary not being eligible.
- cancel_transfer: Requests/questions about canceling a transfer before it’s completed.
- card_payment_fee_charged: Questions/issues about fees charged for card payments.
- card_payment_not_recognised: Unrecognized/out-of-place card transactions, or suspected fraud regarding card use.
- declined_cash_withdrawal: Withdrawal of cash at ATM declined or unsuccessful.
- exchange_rate: Questions/complaints about currency exchange rates or calculations.
- exchange_via_app: Performing currency exchange actions in the app (not ATM or card-related).
- failed_transfer: Problems where a transfer attempt is made but fails or gets rejected for general/unexplained reasons (not related to beneficiary or pending states).
- get_disposable_virtual_card: Requesting or accessing one-time/virtual cards.
- get_physical_card: Requesting or issues with a physical card (not virtual).
- passcode_forgotten: Forgotten/lost passcode or needing assistance recovering access.
- pending_top_up: A top-up was initiated and is still processing/not cleared.
- pending_transfer: A transfer (to another account) is initiated but still awaiting completion or is delayed (not failed or cancelled).
- receiving_money: Questions about receiving, being paid, or accepting inbound transfers (including salary, transfers in other currencies, or if another person can send/credit the account).
- reverted_card_payment?: Card payments that were reversed or returned to account.
- top_up_by_cash_or_cheque: Topping up balance by depositing cash or cheque specifically.
- top_up_reverted: Top-up deposit reversed, returned, or failed after initially proceeding.
- topping_up_by_card: Topping up your account with a debit/credit card (including others topping up your account with a card).
- transfer_fee_charged: Fees specifically for money transfers (not tied to card payments).
- transfer_into_account: Moving money into your account, including incoming transfers (but NOT questions about who/what can send, which is "receiving_money").
- transfer_timing: Questions about the speed or expected completion time of a transfer (including normal or international processing times).
- verify_my_identity: Questions or requests about verifying your own identity.
- verify_source_of_funds: Questions or requests about verifying where your money has come from.

Decision rules:
- If the question is about being paid or allowed to receive (who can credit me, payments from others), use receiving_money.
- If asking about speed or time to complete a transfer or bank deposit, use transfer_timing.
- If no beneficiary can be added or recipient is disallowed/restricted, choose beneficiary_not_allowed.
- If a top-up is made by card (by self or someone else), select topping_up_by_card. If by cash or cheque, select top_up_by_cash_or_cheque.
- If the balance is not updated after a transfer, choose balance_not_updated_after_bank_transfer unless the context is purely about timing, then use transfer_timing.
- Transfers that are initiated but stuck, delayed, or shown as "pending": use pending_transfer. If payment/top-up (not transfer) is pending, use pending_top_up.
- Only select transfer_into_account for moving money in (not questions on who can send, which is receiving_money).

If unsure, choose the most specific relevant category above the more generic options.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the given text into exactly one of these categories: automatic_top_up, balance_not_updated_after_bank_transfer, beneficiary_not_allowed, cancel_transfer, card_payment_fee_charged, card_payment_not_recognised, declined_cash_withdrawal, exchange_rate, exchange_via_app, failed_transfer, get_disposable_virtual_card, get_physical_card, passcode_forgotten, pending_top_up, pending_transfer, receiving_money, reverted_card_payment?, top_up_by_cash_or_cheque, top_up_reverted, topping_up_by_card, transfer_fee_charged, transfer_into_account, transfer_timing, verify_my_identity, verify_source_of_funds.

Definitions:
- automatic_top_up: Enabling/disabling or issues with scheduled or automatic top-ups.
- balance_not_updated_after_bank_transfer: Balance did not update after a bank transfer—focus is on missing or not-yet-visible money, not on timing.
- beneficiary_not_allowed: Recipient/beneficiary can't be added or is rejected for any reason.
- cancel_transfer: Cancel or undo a transfer before completion.
- card_payment_fee_charged: Fees that were charged specifically for using a card.
- card_payment_not_recognised: Unfamiliar, possibly fraudulent, or unrecognized card payments.
- declined_cash_withdrawal: Unsuccessful or declined cash withdrawals (ATM, teller).
- exchange_rate: Complaints/questions about currency exchange rates or conversions.
- exchange_via_app: User-initiated currency exchange actions through app interface.
- failed_transfer: Transfers that do not complete for generic errors; NOT if rejection is due to the recipient/beneficiary (then use beneficiary_not_allowed); NOT if the transfer is delayed or pending (then use pending_transfer or transfer_timing).
- get_disposable_virtual_card: Requests, questions, or issues about virtual/one-time-use cards.
- get_physical_card: Requesting, receiving, activating, or finding information about your physical card—including its PIN.
- passcode_forgotten: Forgotten, lost, or assistance with account passcode or password, NOT card PIN.
- pending_top_up: Top-ups initiated but not completed, i.e., still processing/pending, NOT ordinary card/cash/cheque top-up unless "pending."
- pending_transfer: Transfer is initiated and currently pending, not complete—delay acknowledged; use regardless of questions about timing IF the transfer is explicitly pending.
- receiving_money: Being paid, accepting money, or questions about who/what can send you money.
- reverted_card_payment?: Card payments that were reversed, refunded, or returned.
- top_up_by_cash_or_cheque: Topping up account using physical cash or cheque only.
- top_up_reverted: Money added via top-up that was later reversed, returned, or failed (covers after it initially looked successful).
- topping_up_by_card: Adding money via debit or credit card, including another person doing so.
- transfer_fee_charged: Fees charged specifically for money transfers (other than card activity).
- transfer_into_account: Moving money into your own account (not about sender eligibility—use receiving_money for that).
- transfer_timing: Asking how long, or about the speed, duration, ETA, or when a transfer (or bank transfer) will arrive—only use if the balance is not yet updated AND the main question is about expected timing, not missing money.
- verify_my_identity: Any aspect of verifying/proving your own identity.
- verify_source_of_funds: Explaining, verifying, or justifying where your money comes from.

Key Decision Rules:
- If the question is about being able to receive, or who can pay you, use receiving_money.
- If the main user concern is when funds will arrive (speed/duration/ETA) and NOT that the balance is missing unexpectedly, use transfer_timing.
- If user says a transfer/bank transfer is made and not reflected—USE balance_not_updated_after_bank_transfer, unless timing is their clear main focus (then use transfer_timing).
- If transfer is delayed or stuck processing (not completed), USE pending_transfer; if about processing time and status is "pending," use pending_transfer.
- If top-up is stuck or not credited yet, use pending_top_up (as opposed to other top-up categories).
- If beneficiary/recipient can't be added/chosen/sent to, use beneficiary_not_allowed, even if the app says there’s a generic error.
- If unable to find/see a card PIN or asks "Where is my PIN?", choose get_physical_card (PIN = card PIN). For passcodes/password resets, use passcode_forgotten.
- If money was topped up by card, use topping_up_by_card. By cash/cheque, use top_up_by_cash_or_cheque.
- If transfer is reversed or failed after initially posting, use top_up_reverted (for top-ups) or reverted_card_payment? (for card payments).
- For generic transfer failures where reason is unclear/not about beneficiary/pending state, use failed_transfer.
- If multiple categories seem possible, choose the most specific. Prefer "pending" or "reverted" labels over ordinary ones if the situation is not completed or was reversed.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
